novel_features.name: Rate-quote history compare
novel_features.description: Replay persisted rate quotes to answer 'what would carrier B have cost on the labels carrier A actually shipped?' across any date range.
novel_features.rationale: Rate quotes are ephemeral in the ShipStation API. Once a label is bought, the alternate quotes are gone forever. Persisting every rate call locally is the only way to answer historical cost-comparison questions.
novel_features.example: shipstation-pp-cli rate-history compare --from 2026-04-01 --to 2026-04-30 --carrier ups_ground --vs fedex_home_delivery --json --select carrier,total_cost,delta
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when a finance or ops stakeholder asks 'what would we have saved on UPS vs FedEx last month?' without re-quoting hundreds of stale shipments.

novel_features.name: Batch-error triage
novel_features.description: Lists every batch with unresolved errored sub-shipments, grouped by error reason and aged so you can fix the worst things first.
novel_features.rationale: The web UI shows one batch error at a time. Aggregating errors across all open batches by reason and age requires a local SQL view the API doesn't provide.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this any time a warehouse asks 'which batches still have errors older than X hours?' — it replaces babysitting the errors tab.

novel_features.name: Batch-error retry (errored-only)
novel_features.description: Re-process only the errored sub-shipments in a batch, instead of re-running the whole thing.
novel_features.rationale: ShipStation's batch process endpoint runs the entire batch again. Skimming just the failed sub-shipments from a local error log and re-submitting them is a common-but-tedious manual workflow.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when 4 of 150 sub-shipments fail and you want to retry exactly those 4 without re-paying the postage on the 146 that succeeded.

novel_features.name: Label cost roll-up
novel_features.description: Pivot all labels by carrier / service / weight band with totals, averages, and week-over-week deltas.
novel_features.rationale: The ShipStation API returns one label at a time. Aggregating cost across thousands of labels with grouping is a local SQL aggregate that no MCP or wrapper exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for the weekly CFO shipping-spend report — pipe the CSV straight into Sheets.

novel_features.name: Inventory drift
novel_features.description: Diff ShipStation inventory levels against an external truth file (OMS export, supplier catalog) and print every drifted SKU.
novel_features.rationale: Drift detection requires comparing two sources. The API exposes one source; the user supplies the other via a CSV/JSONL file.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use Tuesday morning to surface SKUs where ShipStation and the OMS disagree, before stockouts cause oversells.

novel_features.name: SKU velocity / stockout risk
novel_features.description: Joins last 30 days of shipped units against current on-hand inventory to compute days-of-cover per SKU and flag stockout risk.
novel_features.rationale: Velocity requires joining shipments to inventory and computing a moving window. ShipStation has no velocity endpoint; the web UI shows neither.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use Friday afternoon to see which SKUs run out before next week's PO arrives.

novel_features.name: End-of-day burndown
novel_features.description: Today's shipments with no label, grouped by warehouse and age, so you can clear the floor before pickup.
novel_features.rationale: Local left-anti-join of shipments not in labels for today's date. The API can list both but cannot join them server-side.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run at 4:30pm before the carrier pickup to confirm every order on the floor has a label printed.

novel_features.name: Orphan finder
novel_features.description: Find shipments stuck without a label after N hours, plus optionally cross-check an OMS-side external-ID list against ShipStation shipments.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-source reconciliation needs both sides of the join. The API has neither concept of 'stuck' nor an external-ID-list filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use any time an integration goes quiet — orphans tells you exactly which orders are missing or stalled.

narrative.headline: Every ShipStation v2 endpoint plus rate-quote history, batch-error triage, cost roll-ups, and inventory velocity that no MCP or web UI surfaces.
narrative.value_prop: Wraps the entire 101-endpoint ShipStation API v2 with a local SQLite store so you can answer questions the live API cannot. `rate-history compare` replays persisted quotes; `batches triage` aggregates open errors by reason and age; `inventory velocity` flags stockout risk before it happens. Agent-native output (`--json`, `--select`, typed exit codes) and offline replay come standard.
narrative.auth_narrative: Uses a single header `api-key`. Set `SHIPSTATION_API_KEY` in your environment (the same env var the official MCP uses) or pass `--api-key` per call. v1 legacy auth (key + secret over Basic) is not supported; this CLI targets the v2 ShipStation API at api.shipstation.com.

quickstart.comment: Verifies the API key reaches api.shipstation.com and reports rate-limit budget.
Confirms which carriers are connected; small payload, fast first call.
Pulls every shipment, label, rate quote, and batch from the last 30 days into the local SQLite store.
Answers 'what would FedEx have cost on every UPS Ground label last month?' without re-quoting.
Lists every batch with unresolved errors older than two hours, grouped by error reason.

troubleshoots.fix: ShipStation rate limit hit. Default is 200 req/min on production, 20 on sandbox. Re-run after the seconds in `Retry-After`, or pass `--rate-limit 100` to throttle preemptively.
The `SHIPSTATION_API_KEY` env var is missing, expired, or pointing at the wrong environment. Generate a new key in app settings; verify with `shipstation-pp-cli doctor`.
Rates persist only when you run `rates calculate` or `rates estimate` through this CLI. Run a few rate calls first, or run `sync --resources rates` if you have realized rates from labels.

troubleshoots.symptom: `batches retry --only-errored` aborts with 'no errored sub-shipments found'
troubleshoots.fix: Run `batches list-errors <batch-id>` first to populate the local batch_errors table. Errors are only fetched once when you list them.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need to script ShipStation operations from a shell, run a Claude Code agent against a real shipping account, or run analytics that the web UI cannot answer. It is best at: rate-shopping with historical comparison, bulk label burst with error triage, inventory drift detection across an OMS, and end-of-day reconciliation before a carrier pickup. Skip it if you just need to print one label \u2014 the web UI is faster.

recipes.title: Compare every UPS Ground label this month against FedEx
recipes.explanation: Joins persisted UPS labels with the closest stored FedEx quotes; `--select` keeps the response compact for agents to parse.

recipes.title: Find every batch with errors older than 2 hours
recipes.explanation: Local SQL over the batch_errors table; sorts by oldest unresolved error so you fix the worst things first.

recipes.title: Re-process only the errored sub-shipments in a batch
recipes.explanation: Reads errored sub-shipment IDs from the local batch_errors table; `--dry-run` shows what would re-process without spending postage.

recipes.title: Generate Friday's CFO shipping-spend report
recipes.command: shipstation-pp-cli labels cost --by carrier,service --week current --csv > shipping-spend.csv
recipes.explanation: Pivots every label cut this week by carrier and service; pipe straight into Sheets for the CFO.

recipes.title: Surface stockout-risk SKUs (narrowing a verbose response)
recipes.explanation: Velocity calls return a row per SKU per warehouse; `--select` narrows the deeply-nested payload to the five fields agents need.

recipes.title: Find orphan orders missing from ShipStation
recipes.explanation: Left-joins your OMS order list against ShipStation shipments; surfaces both stuck shipments and missing-from-ShipStation orders.
